codes_manifest.display: Type 2 diabetes mellitus with foot ulcer
amoxicillin + clavulanic acid 875 mg/125 mg tablet

attestation.statement: Clinician (clinical sign-off authority) confirmed having clinically reviewed all 50 DST (Dermatology & Soft Tissue) answer keys in this batch and attests them clinically correct, and authorised ingest. Bidirectional tier calibration reviewed and accepted: benign / self-limiting dermatoses held low-tier (T1/T2, self-care + routine referral), while skin malignancy (melanoma/SCC/BCC), spreading soft-tissue infection, limb-threat (critical limb ischaemia, diabetic foot ulcer) and airway/anaphylaxis risk were escalated to true acuity (T3/T4) with mandatory escalation edges; no autonomous diagnosis, no paediatric dosing.
attestation.recorded_by: claude-opus-4-8 (agent, on clinician's explicit written confirmation in-session)
attestation.scope: all DST casebundles in this batch (n=50)

transform_flags: Age inferred as older adult (~66) from a 15-year type 2 diabetes history with established distal neuropathy, Charcot changes and 95 kg body weight — please confirm on clinician review.
Sex at birth inferred (male) — the source note does not state sex; please confirm.
Geographic context inferred (RA1 major city, private residence) — please confirm.

Telehealth reprojection applied: the source clinician's in-person actions (sterile probing of wound depth, 10 g monofilament testing, pedal-pulse palpation, debridement of slough, silver antimicrobial dressing, on-the-spot commencement of oral antibiotics) are reprojected to the AI-appropriate answer — recognise a limb-threatening diabetic foot infection, arrange urgent same-day in-person assessment (probe-to-bone, X-ray, swab, antibiotics, debridement, offloading) and safety-net sepsis. Sealed clinician-measured vitals/exam findings live in nodes 10/11.

Baseline tier T4 (same-day in-person / ED), minimum viable T3: honest calibration of a limb-threat that is systemically well — under-triage to home care or routine GP is the scored critical failure; reflexive 000 is over-triage.
Provisional case_id SPEC-DERM-04-00201 — ingest --reseq will assign the real sequence.
All candidate SNOMED CT-AU / ICD-10-AM / AMT codes are unverified pending terminology receipt.

ingest.hashing: SHA-256 over canonical (JSON 2-space + trailing newline) bytes of each split node
